title: Government casts doubt on its own authority to vet AI models - TCR 07/09/26
description:
OpenAI says GPT-5.6 reaches the public Thursday after Commerce Department testing while the White House denies holding any release authority, DeepSeek is designing its own inference chip as Chinese firms shift 46% of accelerator budgets to domestic silicon, and Forterra disclosed 100-plus autonomous ground vehicles running 1,100 missions across nine months in Ukraine.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-july-9-2026/

0:00 Who controls when GPT-5.6 reaches the public
0:30 The Release Nobody Will Claim to Authorize
3:11 DeepSeek moves into silicon as China leaves Nvidia
5:23 Meta ships Muse Image and the consent backlash
8:24 Humanoid robots complete surgical tasks
11:14 A General Machine Does a Specialist's Job
13:56 Closing and Synthesis

This episode covers the GPT-5.6 release-authority dispute, DeepSeek's in-house inference chip and China's move off Nvidia, Meta's Muse Image and its opt-out consent backlash, Forterra's autonomous battlefield vehicles, humanoid robots completing laparoscopic surgery in a Nature study, and GPT-4 predicting social-science experiment outcomes it never saw.

OpenAI framed Thursday's public GPT-5.6 launch as clearance after federal testing, and the White House stated on the record that it approved nothing and holds no release switch - a contradiction that reveals the frontier release process has no settled location, with the review structure forming around models already out the door. The same inversion ran through the hardware: DeepSeek is designing its own inference chip to route around export controls, and a Bloomberg Intelligence survey found Chinese executives plan to move 46% of accelerator budgets to domestic silicon, turning one chokepoint into two supply chains. Meta shipped Muse Image free across its apps with an @-mention feature that pulls any public account into a stranger's edit on opt-out terms, drawing immediate consent backlash. Embodiment crossed into consequence too: Forterra's autonomous vehicles ran 1,100 missions and 52 casualty evacuations across Ukrainian supply lines, and a Nature study showed humanoid robots completing laparoscopic tasks - including a live gallbladder removal on a pig - with a surgeon's own instruments. A separate Nature paper found GPT-4 predicting the outcomes of 70 survey experiments as accurately as pooled human forecasters.
